title: steinhauera sempervirens
intro: Steinhauera sempervirens, also known as bitter melon or bengal melon, is a type of vine-creeping plant that belongs to the Cucurbitaceae family, native to Africa and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Steinhauera sempervirens include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. Steinhauera sempervirens is commonly prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional cooking as a vegetable.
